# Kinetiq — full reference

> A React component library where every animation shares five calibrated springs. Copy the source. Own the code. Ship interfaces that feel machined.

Machine catalog: https://kinetiqui.vercel.app/registry-meta.json · MCP server: https://kinetiqui.vercel.app/mcp

## Operating rules (AGENTS.md)

# Kinetiq — Agent Rules

Operating rules for coding agents working with Kinetiq components. Follow
these and anything you compose stays on the system's motion vocabulary.

## The motion language: five calibrated springs

Every animation uses exactly one of five springs from `lib/motion.ts`. Never
hand-write stiffness/damping numbers — reach for a named spring.

| Spring | stiffness / damping / mass | Use it for |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `flick` | 1100 / 55 / 0.7 | Press states, tick draws, focus. **Confirms.** |
| `snap` | 640 / 42 / 1.0 | Toggles, tab indicators, menus. One crisp overshoot. **Switches.** |
| `glide` | 300 / 34 / 1.0 | Layout shifts, reorders, morphs. **Moves.** |
| `drift` | 120 / 24 / 1.2 | Large surfaces, ambient motion, parallax settle. **Breathes.** |
| `recoil` | 520 / 24 / 1.0 | Toasts, stamps, landings. Two visible bounces. **Celebrates.** |

Say the verb the interaction performs — confirm, switch, move, breathe,
celebrate — and you have the spring.

## Tweens, not springs, for surface properties

Springs drive spatial change (position, scale, rotation). Opacity, color,
blur, and clip use the tween scale from `lib/motion.ts`:

- Durations: `blink 0.08` · `fast 0.15` · `base 0.24` · `slow 0.40` · `page 0.70`
- Easings: `enter [0.22,1,0.36,1]` · `exit [0.5,0,0.75,0]` · `move [0.65,0,0.35,1]` · `linear`
- Enter offsets: `nudge 4` · `step 8` · `shift 16` (px). Scale-ins start at 0.96, never 0.

## Orchestration

- **600ms budget.** A choreographed sequence never exceeds 600ms total. Use
  `cascade(count)` for the stagger interval — it tightens automatically as the
  count grows.
- **Exits are tweens at 0.6x the enter duration** with the `exit` easing, via
  `exitFor()`. Never spring an exit — it reads as indecision.
- One spatial axis per element; stagger from the element the user touched.

## Reduced motion is a first-class state

Read `useMotionSafe()` — never call `matchMedia` directly. When it returns
false, degrade, do not disable:

- Transform enters → opacity fade at `fast`.
- Discrete springs → instant position + a color tween.
- Direct manipulation (drag, sliders) → keep 1:1 tracking; drop inertia and settle.
- Autoplay loops → static (marquees become a wrapped grid).
- Scroll-linked → final state, opacity only. Number rolls → instant value + highlight.

## Tokens

Use shadcn semantic tokens only: `bg-background/card/popover/primary/muted/accent/destructive`,
`text-*-foreground`, `border-border/input`, `ring`. For live-data accents
(readouts, traces, LIVE badges) use `var(--signal, var(--primary))`. Radii:
`rounded-1` (2px) · `rounded-2` (6px) · `rounded-3` (10px) · `rounded-4` (16px).

## Accessibility

Every interactive component ships real WAI-ARIA roles, complete keyboard
support, and visible focus rings (`:focus-visible`, never removed). Silent
state changes announce through an sr-only live region.

## Installing

Components are distributed as source through a shadcn-compatible registry.

```sh
# One-time: register the namespace
npx shadcn@latest registry add @kinetiq=https://kinetiqui.vercel.app/r/{name}.json
# Then add by name
npx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/pressure-button
# Or zero-config by URL
npx shadcn@latest add https://kinetiqui.vercel.app/r/pressure-button.json
```

Installed components import shared helpers (`lib/motion`, `lib/utils`, the
`use-motion-safe` hook) via your project's aliases — the CLI rewrites them on
install. Don't edit those shared files per-component; they're the shared
vocabulary.

## Motion system

Five calibrated springs — every animation uses one of them:

- `springs.flick` — stiffness 1100, damping 55, mass 0.7 (ζ 0.99, settles ~120ms). Press states, tick draws, focus. Confirms.
- `springs.snap` — stiffness 640, damping 42, mass 1 (ζ 0.83, settles ~300ms). Toggles, tab indicators, menus — one crisp overshoot. Switches.
- `springs.glide` — stiffness 300, damping 34, mass 1 (ζ 0.98, settles ~450ms). Layout shifts, reorders, morphs. Moves.
- `springs.drift` — stiffness 120, damping 24, mass 1.2 (ζ 1, settles ~800ms). Large surfaces, ambient drift, parallax settle. Breathes.
- `springs.recoil` — stiffness 520, damping 24, mass 1 (ζ 0.53, settles ~700ms). Toasts, stamps, landings — two visible bounces. Celebrates.

Cascade: Stagger interval = clamp(0.6 / (count - 1), 0.02, 0.06)s so the whole choreography stays under a 600ms budget; dense lists tighten automatically.
Exits: Exits are always tweens at 0.6x the enter duration with the exit easing — springs on exit read as indecision.
Reduced motion: prefers-reduced-motion is a first-class state. Transform enters become opacity fades; discrete springs snap to position with a color tween; direct manipulation keeps 1:1 tracking but drops inertia; autoplay loops go static; scroll-linked renders its final frame; number rolls swap instantly. Every component reads useMotionSafe(), never matchMedia directly.

## Catalog

### Pressure Button (KQ-001) — `pressure-button`
A button that pushes back.

A button that pushes back — press squash on flick, spring rebound on snap, and a hold-to-confirm gauge ring for destructive actions.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/pressure-button`

Props:
- `variant`: "solid" | "outline" | "ghost" | "danger" (default "solid") — Visual style. Danger pairs naturally with holdToConfirm.
- `size`: "sm" | "md" | "lg" (default "md") — Control height and typography scale.
- `holdToConfirm`: number — Milliseconds the button must be held before confirming. Adds a gauge ring; Escape or early release cancels.
- `onConfirm`: () => void — Fires when a hold completes. Hold buttons never fire onClick.

Notes:
- Space and Enter drive the same physics as the pointer — hold either to confirm.
- Under reduced motion the squash is replaced by a shade change; the gauge ring still fills because progress is feedback, not flourish.

### Trace Input (KQ-002) — `trace-input`
Focus draws its own boundary.

Focus draws its own boundary — a stroke traces the field's perimeter, the label glides to an overline, and errors annotate themselves with a dimension line.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/trace-input`

Props:
- `label`: string — Field label — rests as placeholder, floats to overline when focused or filled.
- `description / error`: string — Helper and error lines below the field, chained into aria-describedby. Errors nudge the field and set aria-invalid.
- `prefix / suffix`: ReactNode — Leading and trailing slots inside the field.
- `…input props`: Omit<InputProps, "prefix"> — Standard input passthrough; controlled and uncontrolled both work.

Notes:
- The perimeter trace doubles as the focus indicator — it is never removed, only accelerated.
- Under reduced motion the stroke appears instantly and the error nudge is skipped; the message still announces via aria-invalid.

### Breaker Switch (KQ-003) — `breaker-switch`
A toggle with mechanical conviction.

A toggle with mechanical conviction — the thumb stretches in anticipation while held, throws on snap, and squashes against the track end as the rail recoils.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/breaker-switch`

Props:
- `checked / defaultChecked / onCheckedChange`: boolean / boolean / (checked) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled state.
- `size`: "sm" | "md" | "lg" (default "md") — Fixed geometry — travel is derived, nothing is measured.
- `onLabel / offLabel`: string — Tiny uppercase text inside the track ends.
- `label`: ReactNode — Adjacent clickable label, associated automatically.
- `name / value`: string — Renders a hidden input that submits while checked.

Notes:
- A native button with role="switch" — Space and Enter throw it, and Space arms the anticipation stretch.
- Under reduced motion the thumb repositions instantly; only the color crossfade remains.

### Checkbox (KQ-004) — `checkbox`
The tick is drawn, not shown.

The tick is drawn, not shown — a pathLength stroke on flick, an indeterminate dash that slides in, and group select-alls that cascade down the list.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/checkbox`

Props:
- `checked / defaultChecked`: boolean — Controlled or uncontrolled checked state.
- `indeterminate`: boolean (default false) — Mixed state — dash visual plus native el.indeterminate.
- `onCheckedChange`: (checked: boolean) => void — Fires with the next checked state.
- `label / description`: ReactNode — Visible label (required) and muted secondary line.
- `CheckboxGroup`: items, value, onValueChange, selectAll, legend… — Fieldset-based group with select-all parent (checked / unchecked / indeterminate) and cascading tick draws.

Notes:
- Native hidden inputs do the accessibility work; the drawn box is decorative.
- Under reduced motion ticks appear instantly and select-all cascades lose their stagger.

### Caliper Slider (KQ-005) — `caliper-slider`
Reads like a vernier, moves like grease.

Reads like a vernier, moves like grease — 1:1 drag with a snap settle, ticks that lean toward the jaw, and a live measured-span dimension line in range mode.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/caliper-slider`

Props:
- `min / max / step`: number (default 0 / 100 / 1) — Bounds and snap grid (float-precision cleaned).
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: number | [number, number] — Single or range; live stepped values during drag, deduped.
- `range`: boolean (default false) — Two facing caliper jaws with a live span dimension line.
- `marks`: number[] | boolean (default true) — Every step when ≤ 40 steps, else quarters; or pinned positions.
- `format / readout`: (v) => string / "float" | "end" | "none" — Value formatting and where the mono readout renders.

Notes:
- Full APG slider keyboard set per thumb; range thumbs cross-clamp their ARIA bounds.
- Under reduced motion tracking stays 1:1 — only the tick lean and the settle spring are removed.

### Gantry Tabs (KQ-006) — `gantry-tabs`
The indicator rides a rail.

The indicator rides a rail — its leading edge glides ahead while the trailing edge lags a beat, stretching toward the destination before contracting to fit.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/gantry-tabs`

Props:
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: string / string / (value) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled active tab.
- `variant`: "underline" | "segmented" | "enclosed" (default "underline") — Indicator style.
- `orientation`: "horizontal" | "vertical" (default "horizontal") — Layout and arrow-key axis.
- `activationMode`: "automatic" | "manual" (default "automatic") — Activate on focus, or on Enter/Space.
- `keepMounted`: boolean (default false) — Keep inactive panels mounted but hidden.

Notes:
- Full APG tabs keyboard support with wrapping arrows; disabled triggers are skipped.
- Under reduced motion the indicator teleports and panels crossfade without the lift.

### Drawer Accordion (KQ-007) — `drawer-accordion`
Opens like a specimen cabinet.

Opens like a specimen cabinet — height glides while the inner content lags a beat behind, like paper settling in a pulled drawer.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/drawer-accordion`

Props:
- `type`: "single" | "multiple" (default "single") — Open one drawer at a time, or many.
- `value / defaultValue`: string | string[] — Controlled or uncontrolled open value(s).
- `onValueChange`: (value) => void — Fires with the next open value(s).
- `collapsible`: boolean (default true) — Single mode: allow closing the open item.
- `Item.value / Item.disabled`: string / boolean — Unique drawer id; disabled items are skipped in arrow navigation.
- `Trigger.icon`: ReactNode — Leading icon slot before the header label.

Notes:
- Full APG accordion keyboard support: Arrow keys move between headers, Home and End jump.
- Under reduced motion drawers open instantly and content crossfades — no lag, no sibling nudge.

### Callout (KQ-008) — `callout`
A label with a leader line.

A tooltip with a leader line — the label pops from the anchor while a schematic elbow line draws itself to the card.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/callout`

Props:
- `content`: ReactNode — Label content. Never put interactive elements here.
- `side / align`: "top" | "right" | "bottom" | "left" / "start" | "center" | "end" (default "top" / "center") — Anchor side and cross-axis alignment.
- `delay`: number (default 150) — Hover open delay in ms. Keyboard focus opens instantly.
- `followCursor`: boolean (default false) — Label trails the pointer with drift-damped lag; leader line omitted.
- `disabled`: boolean (default false) — Never opens.

Notes:
- Opens on hover and on keyboard focus; Escape dismisses.
- Under reduced motion the card fades in place — no pop, no leader draw, and followCursor pins to the anchor.

### Morph Dialog (KQ-009) — `morph-dialog`
The trigger becomes the dialog.

The trigger becomes the dialog — a shared-element morph glides the card you clicked into the panel it opens, and reverses on close.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/morph-dialog`

Props:
- `open / defaultOpen / onOpenChange`: boolean / boolean / (open) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled open state.
- `size`: "sm" | "md" | "lg" (default "md") — Panel width preset; ignored by the sheet variant.
- `sheet`: boolean (default false) — Bottom-sheet variant — full-width, pinned to the bottom edge.
- `dismissible`: boolean (default true) — Escape, backdrop click, and the built-in close button.
- `portal`: boolean (default true) — Portal to body, or contain the overlay in a relative parent.
- `Content.title / Content.description`: string — Wired to aria-labelledby and aria-describedby; title renders as h2.

Notes:
- Focus is trapped while open and restored to the trigger on close; body scroll locks in portal mode.
- Under reduced motion the morph becomes a plain centered fade.

### Select (KQ-010) — `select`
A menu that unfolds from where you asked.

A menu that unfolds from where you asked — the panel scales from the trigger's edge, options cascade in, and a highlight bar glides between them.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/select`

Props:
- `items`: (SelectOption | SelectGroup)[] — Flat options, labeled groups, or a mix; options take value, label, description, disabled.
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: string / string / (value) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled selection.
- `searchable`: boolean (default false) — Pins a filter input at the top of the panel.
- `placeholder / label / name`: string — Empty-state text, wired visible label, and hidden form input.

Notes:
- Combobox APG throughout: aria-activedescendant highlight, wrapping arrows, typeahead, Escape restores focus.
- Under reduced motion everything fades — no scale, no cascade, instant highlight bar.

### Telemetry Toast (KQ-011) — `telemetry-toast`
Messages that queue like readings.

Messages that queue like readings — recoil arrivals, a depth-receding stack, swipe dismissal with velocity projection, and timers that pause under your pointer.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/telemetry-toast`

Props:
- `ToastProvider.position`: "bottom-right" | "bottom-left" | "top-right" | "top-left" (default "bottom-right") — Viewport corner for the stack.
- `ToastProvider.max`: number (default 3) — Visible stack size; extras queue FIFO.
- `useToast()`: { toast(input) => id, dismiss(id) } — Imperative API from anywhere under the provider.
- `ToastInput`: title, description?, variant?, duration?, action? — Variants info | success | warn | danger; duration ≤ 0 persists; action renders a button.

Notes:
- Danger toasts announce as alerts; the region is focusable via an Alt+T hotkey chord and timers pause on hover or focus.
- Under reduced motion the stack flattens to a list with fade-only transitions — the progress hairline still drains.

### Readout (KQ-012) — `readout`
Numbers with momentum.

Numbers with momentum — per-digit vertical rolls with real carry propagation, so 199 → 200 visibly carries right to left.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/readout`

Props:
- `value`: number — The numeric value to display.
- `format`: (value: number) => string (default toLocaleString) — Formats the value; non-digit characters render as static cells.
- `size`: "sm" | "md" | "lg" | "xl" (default "md") — Type scale.
- `rollOn`: "any" | "increase" (default "any") — With "increase", decreases swap instantly — right for latency-style metrics.
- `delta`: { value: string; direction: "up" | "down" } — Change badge that flips up when the value moves.

Notes:
- Rolling digits are aria-hidden; a polite live region announces the settled value once per burst.
- Under reduced motion the value swaps instantly with a brief highlight pulse.

### Focus Text (KQ-013) — `focus-text`
Copy that resolves under the lens.

Copy that resolves under the lens — words sharpen from an 8px blur like a microscope focus-pull, cascaded under the 600ms budget.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/focus-text`

Props:
- `children`: string — The copy to resolve.
- `as`: "span" | "div" | "p" | "h1"–"h4" (default "span") — Rendered element.
- `by`: "word" | "char" (default "word") — Split granularity; char mode never breaks mid-word.
- `trigger / once`: "inView" | "mount" / boolean (default "inView" / true) — When the pull runs, and whether it re-blurs out of view.
- `stagger / startDelay`: number — Seconds between units (defaults to the cascade budget) and lead-in delay.

Notes:
- Screen readers get a clean sr-only copy; the animated units are aria-hidden.
- Under reduced motion the block does a single fast fade — text is always readable.

### Flapboard (KQ-014) — `flapboard`
Split-flap departures for your data.

Split-flap departures for your data — each cell flips through intermediate characters with a column cascade, like an airport board settling.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/flapboard`

Props:
- `value`: string — Text to display, normalized against the charset.
- `chars`: string (default " A–Z 0–9 ·-") — Charset scanned in flip order.
- `flipSpeed`: number (default 40) — Milliseconds per flip step.
- `padTo / align`: number / "left" | "right" — Minimum cell count and which side the value sits on.
- `size`: "sm" | "md" | "lg" (default "md") — Fixed tile dimensions.

Notes:
- Cells are aria-hidden; a polite status region announces the settled value once.
- Under reduced motion the board crossfades to the new value — no flipping.

### Scope Scrubber (KQ-015) — `scope-scrubber`
Drag a value; watch its physics.

Drag a value; watch its physics — a scrub input with a live oscilloscope trace behind the numeral, where the settle spring's overshoot draws itself.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/scope-scrubber`

Props:
- `min / max / step`: number — Bounds (required) and release snap grid.
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: number / number / (value) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled; fires live while scrubbing.
- `settle`: SpringName (default "snap") — Which calibrated spring the value settles on — its personality shows on the trace.
- `sensitivity`: number (default (max−min)/200) — Value change per pixel of drag.
- `label / unit / format / trace`: string / string / (v)=>string / boolean — Labeling, suffix, numeral formatting, and the history trace toggle.

Notes:
- Enter or double-click converts the plate into a plain number input; Escape cancels.
- Under reduced motion scrubbing stays 1:1, release snaps instantly, and the trace hides.

### Metronome Loader (KQ-016) — `metronome-loader`
Waiting, kept honest.

Waiting, kept honest — a pendulum, a bearing cradle, or a radar sweep; give it a value and it morphs into a determinate arc gauge.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/metronome-loader`

Props:
- `variant`: "pendulum" | "bearing" | "sweep" (default "pendulum") — Indeterminate style; ignored once value is set.
- `size`: "sm" | "md" | "lg" (default "md") — 24 / 40 / 64 px.
- `value`: number — 0–100; presence morphs the loader into a determinate arc gauge.
- `label / showLabel`: string / boolean — Accessible name; optionally rendered visibly.

Notes:
- role="progressbar" throughout — indeterminate announces Loading, determinate reports its value.
- Under reduced motion the loops freeze to a static glyph with a slow opacity pulse.

### Gyro Card (KQ-017) — `gyro-card`
A card with a sense of balance.

A card with a sense of balance — pointer tilt with real inertia that overshoots past level and rebalances when you leave, with layered parallax and tracking glare.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/gyro-card`

Props:
- `maxTilt`: number (default 8) — Peak rotation toward the pointer, in degrees.
- `glare`: boolean (default true) — Radial sheen that tracks the light angle.
- `GyroLayer.depth`: 0 | 1 | 2 — Parallax depth for child slots — pinned, ±4px, or ±8px counter-translation.

Notes:
- The motion is decorative: DOM order is untouched, and keyboard focus inside flattens the card to an energized ring state.
- Under reduced motion there is no tilt, parallax, or glare — hover becomes a border emphasis.

### Magnet Dock (KQ-018) — `magnet-dock`
Icons that feel the cursor coming.

Icons that feel the cursor coming — a cosine distance field magnifies the dock, and drag-reorder parts neighbors magnetically before snapping into a slot.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/magnet-dock`

Props:
- `items`: DockItem[] — { id, label, icon, active?, onSelect? } per icon.
- `orientation`: "horizontal" | "vertical" (default "horizontal") — Dock axis — pointer tracking, tooltips, and arrows follow.
- `magnify`: number (default 1.6) — Peak scale under the pointer or keyboard focus.
- `reorderable / onReorder`: boolean / (order: string[]) => void — Pointer drag plus Space-lift keyboard reordering, announced live.

Notes:
- role="toolbar" with roving tabindex; a focused icon magnifies without the pointer.
- Under reduced motion magnification is replaced by a 2px raise; reordering still works, 1:1.

### Conveyor List (KQ-019) — `conveyor-list`
A queue that moves like a belt.

A queue that moves like a belt — items drift in from the feed side, brake into their slot with a squash, and lift off the belt when removed.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/conveyor-list`

Props:
- `items / keyFor / renderItem`: T[] / (item) => string / (item) => ReactNode — Generic queue contents with stable keys driving enter, exit, and reorder identity.
- `side`: "top" | "bottom" (default "top") — The feed edge new items enter from.
- `maxVisible`: number (default 6) — Rows shown before the oldest overflow into a +N more count.
- `announceItem`: (item: T) => string — Screen-reader text per row for the live log region.

Notes:
- The list is a role="log" live region — arrivals are announced politely.
- Under reduced motion rows crossfade in place and layout shifts are instant.

### Ticker Tape (KQ-020) — `ticker-tape`
A marquee with friction.

A marquee with friction — hover applies drag, grabbing tracks 1:1, and a fling carries real momentum that decays back to duty speed.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/ticker-tape`

Props:
- `speed`: number (default 60) — Duty cruising speed in px/s.
- `direction`: "left" | "right" (default "left") — Travel direction.
- `gap`: number (default 24) — Gap between items and loop copies.
- `pauseOnHover`: boolean (default true) — Ease to 30% duty speed while hovered.

Notes:
- A visible pause control surfaces on keyboard focus; duplicated sequences are aria-hidden.
- Under reduced motion the tape renders as a static wrapped grid showing each item once.

### Scan Reveal (KQ-021) — `scan-reveal`
Sections develop as the scanner passes.

Sections develop as the scanner passes — a scroll-linked scan line sweeps once, and content resolves beneath it with a coordinate readout riding the beam.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/scan-reveal`

Props:
- `once`: boolean (default true) — Tear down the dual layers after the sweep completes.
- `direction`: "down" | "up" (default "down") — Sweep direction.
- `containerRef`: RefObject<HTMLElement> — Scrollable ancestor for scroll-linking inside overflow containers.

Notes:
- Interactive content lives only in the developed layer; the undeveloped copy is aria-hidden and inert.
- Under reduced motion content renders fully developed with a single fast fade.

### Radio Group (KQ-022) — `radio-group`
Selection that travels, never teleports.

Selection that travels, never teleports — one shared dot glides between rings while the receiving ring arms with a squash and rebounds as it lands.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/radio-group`

Props:
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: string / string / (value) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled selection.
- `orientation`: "vertical" | "horizontal" (default "vertical") — Layout and aria-orientation.
- `name / label`: string / ReactNode — Hidden native radios for form posts; visible group label (or pass aria-label).
- `Item.value / Item.description`: string / ReactNode — Item identity and a muted secondary line.

Notes:
- Full APG radio keyboard: arrows wrap and select, Space selects, Home and End jump.
- Under reduced motion the dot swaps instantly with only a border-color tween.

### Code Cells (KQ-023) — `code-cells`
Six digits, dropped into place.

Six digits, dropped into place — one hidden one-time-code input drives visual cells with digit drops, underline ticks, and a rejection nudge.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/code-cells`

Props:
- `length`: number (default 6) — Cell count; also the hidden input's maxLength.
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: string / string / (code) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled code, digits only.
- `onComplete`: (code: string) => void — Fires once per fill; re-arms when the code shrinks.
- `error / errorMessage`: boolean / string — Destructive borders plus a row nudge and a role=alert line.
- `groups`: number[] — e.g. [3,3] renders a separator between digit groups.
- `ref`: Ref<HTMLInputElement> — Reaches the hidden input — focus() after clearing.

Notes:
- The single hidden input with autocomplete="one-time-code" is the accessibility-correct OTP pattern — cells are decorative.
- Under reduced motion digits appear instantly and the nudge is skipped; the alert still announces.

### Action Relay (KQ-024) — `action-relay`
A button that narrates the job.

A button that narrates the job — state labels roll through a one-line mask with a blur crossfade while the width follows the new content.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/action-relay`

Props:
- `state / states`: string / Record<string, ReactNode> — Controlled state key and the content per state — nodes may embed icons or a small loader.
- `busyStates`: string[] — States that report aria-busy and suppress interaction without stealing focus.
- `onSettled`: (state) => void — Fires when the incoming roll lands — chain done → idle off it.
- `announcements`: Record<string, string> — Screen-reader text per state; plain-text nodes announce themselves.
- `variant / size`: pressure-button scales (default "solid" / "md") — Visual style, mirroring the button family.

Notes:
- Busy states keep focus (aria-disabled, not disabled) so the announcement chain is never interrupted.
- Under reduced motion labels crossfade and the width jumps; onSettled still fires.

### Status Seal (KQ-025) — `status-seal`
Status, re-stamped on every change.

Status, re-stamped on every change — variant swaps stamp in on recoil, a live pulse ring breathes at drift tempo, and counts carry-roll.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/status-seal`

Props:
- `variant`: "info" | "success" | "warn" | "danger" (default "info") — Semantic tinting with token fallbacks for consumer apps.
- `children / icon`: ReactNode — Label and optional leading node (defaults to a status dot).
- `count`: number — Embeds a small Readout so numbers carry-roll without re-stamping.
- `live`: boolean (default false) — Pulse ring behind the dot at drift tempo.

Notes:
- role="status" announces label and count once per change, debounced.
- Under reduced motion swaps are instant and the pulse ring holds static at mid-opacity.

### Slipstream (KQ-026) — `slipstream`
A highlight that rides in your wake.

A highlight that rides in your wake — one pill glides behind hovered or focused items with a trailing-edge stretch, in any direction.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/slipstream`

Props:
- `radius / inset`: number / number (default 8 / 0) — Pill corner radius, and how far it tucks inside each item rect (negative bleeds out).
- `disabled`: boolean (default false) — Parks the pill and ignores hover/focus.
- `SlipstreamItem`: div props — Wrap each link or button; the wrapper registers its rect for the pill to target.

Notes:
- Keyboard focus drives the pill exactly like hover — focusin on the group targets the focused item.
- Under reduced motion the pill teleports with a fast fade; no trailing stretch.

### Drawer (KQ-027) — `drawer`
A side panel on rails.

A side panel on rails — slides in on glide, drags toward its edge 1:1, and a projected release past 40% lets it leave with the momentum it already has.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/drawer`

Props:
- `open / defaultOpen / onOpenChange`: boolean / boolean / (open) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled visibility.
- `side / size`: "left" | "right" / "sm" | "md" | "lg" (default "right" / "md") — Edge and width preset (288 / 360 / 440).
- `dismissible / portal`: boolean (default true / true) — Escape, backdrop, and edge-drag dismissal; portal locks body scroll.
- `Trigger / Content / Title / Description / Close`: compound parts — Composable parts sharing one context.

Notes:
- The backdrop's opacity is bound to the drag — dismissal fades exactly as far as you've pulled.
- Under reduced motion the panel fades; dragging stays 1:1 with instant resolution.

### Bottom Sheet (KQ-028) — `bottom-sheet`
It snaps where your gesture was going.

It snaps where your gesture was going — velocity-projected releases settle on the nearest snap point in the gesture's direction, with rubber-band overdrag.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/bottom-sheet`

Props:
- `snapPoints / initialSnap / onSnapChange`: number[] / number / (index) => void (default [0.4, 0.9] / 0) — Resting heights as viewport fractions; every settle reports its index.
- `open / defaultOpen / onOpenChange`: boolean / boolean / (open) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled visibility.
- `dismissible / portal`: boolean (default true / true) — Projecting past the lowest snap dismisses; portal measures the visual viewport.
- `Handle`: compound part — The grab pill is a real button — arrows step snaps, Home/End jump, each step announces.

Notes:
- The backdrop opacity is bound to the sheet's travel, live during the drag.
- Under reduced motion it fades to its snap and releases resolve instantly; dragging stays 1:1.

### Cipher Text (KQ-029) — `cipher-text`
Characters lock in from the noise.

Characters lock in from the noise — glyphs cycle deterministically, then resolve left-to-right under the 600ms cascade budget with a signal pulse per lock.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/cipher-text`

Props:
- `children`: string — Copy to resolve; split into per-character mono cells.
- `trigger`: "inView" | "mount" | "hover" (default "inView") — Hover re-scrambles then resolves on every entry.
- `order`: "ltr" | "random" (default "ltr") — Lock-in sequence; random is hash-shuffled, never Math.random.
- `charset / speed / as`: string / number / element (default A–Z 0–9 ·- / 30 / "span") — Glyph pool, ms per scramble tick, rendered element.

Notes:
- Screen readers and no-JS visitors get the clean string; the scramble layer is aria-hidden.
- Under reduced motion the text renders plainly with a single fast fade.

### Ledger (KQ-030) — `ledger`
Ten thousand rows, sixty frames.

Ten thousand rows, sixty frames — a hand-rolled virtualized table where sorting FLIPs only the rows you can see and selection cascades down the window.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/ledger`

Props:
- `columns / rows / rowId`: LedgerColumn<T>[] / readonly T[] / (row) => string — Column defs (width px | "1fr", align, sortable, cell, sortFn), the full data set, and stable row identity.
- `rowHeight / height / overscan`: number (default 40 / 320 / 6) — Fixed row height, viewport height, and window padding.
- `sort / defaultSort / onSortChange`: LedgerSort | null — Controlled or uncontrolled; headers cycle asc → desc → none.
- `selectable / selected / onSelectedChange`: boolean / ReadonlySet<string> / (next) => void — Checkbox column; select-all targets every row, not just visible.
- `onVisibleRangeChange`: ({ from, to }) => void — 1-based visible range, emitted from the rAF scroll flush.

Notes:
- Roughly twenty nodes render regardless of row count; sort animates at most thirty visible rows.
- Under reduced motion the chevron swaps instantly and rows jump — no FLIP, no tick cascade.

### Zoetrope (KQ-031) — `zoetrope`
Spin the drum; every frame finds its detent.

Spin the drum; every frame finds its detent — panels ride the inside of a cylinder with drag inertia, wheel steps, and angular-distance dimming.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/zoetrope`

Props:
- `children`: ReactNode — Each direct child becomes a drum face (capped at twelve).
- `index / defaultIndex / onIndexChange`: number / number / (index) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled front panel; fires on every settled detent.
- `itemWidth / itemHeight / perspective`: number (default 160 / 200 / 1000) — Panel geometry; the ring radius derives from width and count.
- `sensitivity`: number (default 0.35) — Drag ratio in degrees per pixel.
- `getLabel`: (index) => string — Names panels for the live announcer.

Notes:
- Only the fronted panel is interactive; the rest are inert and aria-hidden.
- Under reduced motion the drum becomes a flat scroll-snap row with identical semantics.

### Wavefield (KQ-032) — `wavefield`
Ambient physics, drawn on canvas.

Ambient physics, drawn on canvas — interference lattices, drifting contours, breathing grids, and particle fields that pause when unseen.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/wavefield`

Props:
- `variant`: "interference" | "contour" | "lattice" | "drift" (default "interference") — Which field is drawn.
- `speed / density / opacity`: number (default 0.5 / 0.5 / 1) — Clock rate, geometry density (capped), and canvas-layer opacity.
- `children`: ReactNode — Overlay slot above the canvas — it carries all the semantics.

Notes:
- Colors resolve from your theme tokens and re-resolve on theme flips; the loop pauses when hidden or offscreen.
- Under reduced motion exactly one designed frame is drawn — the loop never starts.

### Phase Switch (KQ-033) — `phase-switch`
Day to night in one clean sweep.

Day to night in one clean sweep — a framework-agnostic theme toggle that reveals the new theme as a circle growing from the button, via the View Transitions API.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/phase-switch`

Props:
- `checked / onCheckedChange`: boolean / (next) => void — Controlled only — flip your own theme class inside the callback; the component owns zero theme logic.
- `size / label`: "sm" | "md" | "lg" / string (default "md" / "Toggle theme") — Control size and accessible name.

Notes:
- Add `::view-transition-old(root), ::view-transition-new(root) { animation: none }` to your CSS so the clip reveal isn't fought by the default crossfade.
- Unsupported browsers and reduced motion get an instant flip with an icon crossfade.

### Kinetic Gallery (KQ-035) — `kinetic-gallery`
Throw the strip; the nearest frame finds the rail.

Grab the strip and throw it — release carries momentum that decays and snaps the nearest frame to the rail, with prev/next and dot controls.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/kinetic-gallery`

Props:
- `children`: ReactNode — Each direct child is a slide; widths are measured for snapping.
- `gap`: number (default 16) — Pixel gap between slides.
- `align`: "start" | "center" (default "start") — Where the active slide settles against the rail.
- `aria-label`: string — Names the gallery region for assistive tech.

Notes:
- Arrow keys step slides, Home and End jump to the ends; a live region announces the current frame.
- Under reduced motion it becomes a native scroll-snap strip — direct swipe, no thrown momentum.

### Tile Grid (KQ-036) — `tile-grid`
Drag a tile; the grid reflows around it.

Drag a tile and the grid reflows around it — the others FLIP-glide into their new slots, with a full keyboard lift-move-drop for reordering without a mouse.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/tile-grid`

Props:
- `tiles`: { id: string; content: ReactNode }[] — The tiles in their initial order; identity drives the FLIP.
- `columns`: number (default 3) — Grid column count.
- `onOrderChange`: (ids: string[]) => void — Fires with the new id order after a drop or keyboard move.
- `aria-label`: string — Names the reorderable list.

Notes:
- Keyboard: Space lifts a tile, arrows move it (left/right by one, up/down by a row), Space drops, Escape cancels.
- Under reduced motion tiles snap to their new positions — dragging still tracks 1:1, without the glide.

### Segmented Control (KQ-037) — `segmented-control`
A thumb that glides to the chosen segment.

A compact value picker whose raised thumb glides between segments on snap — radio-group semantics, so it selects a value rather than switching panels.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/segmented-control`

Props:
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: string / string / (value) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled selection.
- `name / label`: string / ReactNode — Hidden native radios for form posts; a visible group label (or pass aria-label).
- `size`: "sm" | "md" (default "md") — Control height and type scale.
- `Item.value / Item.disabled`: string / boolean — Segment identity and per-segment disabling.

Notes:
- Full APG radio keyboard: arrows move and select (wrapping), Home and End jump, Space selects.
- Under reduced motion the thumb appears under the active segment instantly — no travel, color only.

### Triage Deck (KQ-038) — `triage-deck`
Flick to accept, flick to reject.

A decision stack you flick through — throw a card right to accept or left to reject, a velocity-projected release commits, and the next card rises from behind.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/triage-deck`

Props:
- `cards`: { id: string; content: ReactNode }[] — The queue; the top card is interactive, the rest peek behind.
- `onDecide`: (id, decision: "accept" | "reject") => void — Fires when a card commits in either direction.
- `onEmpty`: () => void — Fires when the last card is decided.
- `acceptLabel / rejectLabel`: string / string — Button and affordance labels.

Notes:
- Arrow keys decide (left rejects, right accepts) and buttons drive it pointer-free; a live region announces each decision.
- Under reduced motion cards cross-fade instead of flying off — dragging still tracks, without the throw.

### Spark Chart (KQ-039) — `spark-chart`
A trace that draws itself, then reads out.

A compact line or area chart that draws itself on arrival, then lets you scrub a crosshair that snaps to the nearest point and reads its value.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/spark-chart`

Props:
- `data`: number[] | { x: number | string; y: number }[] — Bare numbers get index labels; objects carry their own x.
- `variant`: "line" | "area" (default "line") — Stroke only, or a filled area under the curve.
- `height / format`: number / (y) => string — Chart height in px and value formatting for the readout.
- `label`: string — Accessible name and summary for the trace.

Notes:
- Focus and arrow-key to move the crosshair; Home and End jump; the active point is announced.
- Under reduced motion the trace renders fully drawn — scrubbing still works, without the draw-on.

### Radial Bars (KQ-040) — `radial-bars`
Bars that radiate from a hub.

A polar bar chart — wedges radiate from a hub, each growing to its value on the glide spring; hover or focus a wedge to brighten it and read its value in the hub.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/radial-bars`

Props:
- `data`: { label: string; value: number; color?: string }[] — One wedge per entry; value drives its radial length.
- `max`: number — Value that reaches the outer radius; defaults to the data max.
- `size / format`: number / (value) => string — Diameter in px and value formatting for the readout.
- `aria-label`: string — Names the chart region.

Notes:
- Every wedge is a focusable data point announcing its label and value, so the chart is operable by keyboard.
- Under reduced motion the wedges render at full length instantly — the hover/focus highlight is color only.

### Coverflow (KQ-041) — `coverflow`
Covers that bank away in perspective.

A cover gallery in perspective — the active card faces you while its neighbors bank away and recede, and any card you steer to glides to the front.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/coverflow`

Props:
- `children`: ReactNode — Each direct child is a cover.
- `index / defaultIndex / onIndexChange`: number / number / (index) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled active cover.
- `aria-label`: string — Names the gallery region.

Notes:
- Drag, wheel, click a side cover, or arrow-key to steer; a live region announces the active item.
- Under reduced motion it flattens to a native scroll-snap row — no perspective, same controls.

### Parallax Scene (KQ-042) — `parallax-scene`
Layers that part around the pointer.

A depth-layered diorama that parades its layers past the pointer — each sits at its own depth, the whole scene tilts toward your cursor and drifts back on leave.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/parallax-scene`

Props:
- `children`: ReactNode — ParallaxLayer elements, back to front (max ~4).
- `maxTilt`: number (default 8) — Degrees the scene rotates toward the pointer.
- `Layer.depth`: number — 0 is far and moves least; 1 is near and moves most.

Notes:
- The parallax is decorative — layer content stays in normal flow and fully focusable.
- Under reduced motion the layers render flat and stationary at their neutral position.

### Tether Rope (KQ-043) — `tether-rope`
Grab the cord; Verlet does the rest.

A hanging rope you grab and swing — a Verlet chain with gravity and length constraints, so it whips, goes taut, and settles like real cord.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/tether-rope`

Props:
- `nodes`: number (default 12) — Segment nodes, clamped to 4–16.
- `anchor`: "top" | "ends" (default "top") — Pin the first node, or both ends like a slung cable.
- `height`: number (default 280) — Stage height in px.

Notes:
- Drag any point to swing the rope; a Reset button re-drops it. The loop pauses offscreen and when the tab is hidden.
- Under reduced motion it renders a static hanging catenary — no simulation.

### Pendulum Wave (KQ-044) — `pendulum-wave`
Periods that part, then realign.

A rank of pendulums whose periods step apart — released together they drift through traveling waves and snap back into alignment on a fixed cycle.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/pendulum-wave`

Props:
- `count`: number (default 12) — Pendulums, clamped to 4–20.
- `amplitude / cycleSeconds / baseOscillations`: number — Swing angle, realign-cycle length, and the longest pendulum's swing count.
- `height`: number (default 260) — Stage height in px.

Notes:
- Motion is analytic — each bob follows a closed-form cosine, so the realign cycle is exact. Restart re-aligns them.
- Under reduced motion it renders one static fanned frame — no loop.

### Rubber Sheet (KQ-045) — `rubber-sheet`
Pull the membrane; it rings back.

A taut mesh you pull toward the pointer with a smooth falloff; release and the vertices fire home, ringing through equilibrium twice before they settle.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/rubber-sheet`

Props:
- `columns / rows`: number — Mesh density; columns default 9, rows default 7.
- `height`: number (default 260) — Stage height in px.
- `aria-label`: string — Names the surface.

Notes:
- The release rides the recoil spring, so the sheet overshoots and settles in two visible bounces.
- Under reduced motion the mesh deforms 1:1 while dragging but snaps flat on release — no ripple.

### Iron Filings (KQ-046) — `iron-filings`
A field that aligns to your cursor.

A canvas lattice of dash filings that align to the cursor like iron around a magnetic pole — near the pointer they snap radial and brighten, far off they relax and dim.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/iron-filings`

Props:
- `spacing`: number (default 26) — Pixels between filings in the grid (clamped 16–48).
- `height`: number (default 320) — Stage height in px when used standalone.
- `children`: ReactNode — Overlay content rendered above the field.

Notes:
- The canvas loop pauses offscreen and when the tab is hidden; colors re-resolve on a theme flip.
- Under reduced motion it renders one static lattice frame — no loop, no cursor response.

### Swarm Field (KQ-047) — `swarm-field`
A flock that scatters from your cursor.

A canvas flock of boids that murmur with emergent flocking and scatter from the cursor, then regroup — separation, alignment, cohesion, and flee, on a spatial hash.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/swarm-field`

Props:
- `count`: number (default 80) — Boid count, clamped to 10–100.
- `height`: number (default 320) — Stage height in px when used standalone.
- `children`: ReactNode — Overlay content rendered above the field.

Notes:
- Neighbor queries run on a spatial hash, so the flock stays near O(n); the loop pauses offscreen and when hidden.
- Under reduced motion it renders one settled frame — no loop, no scatter.

### Magnetic Cursor (KQ-048) — `magnetic-cursor`
Targets that reach for the cursor.

A pointer layer for fine cursors — a drift-lagged spotlight trails the cursor and any MagneticTarget leans toward it, then springs back; inert on touch and reduced motion.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/magnetic-cursor`

Props:
- `MagneticCursor.size`: number (default 28) — Spotlight follower diameter in px.
- `MagneticTarget.strength`: number (default 0.35) — Fraction of the pointer offset a target follows.
- `MagneticTarget.radius`: number (default 90) — Activation radius in px around the target.

Notes:
- Wrap real controls in MagneticTarget — the transform layer never touches focus or clicks.
- Active only on (pointer:fine) with motion allowed; otherwise children render untouched.

### Aurora Ribbon (KQ-049) — `aurora-ribbon`
Light ribbons that lean toward the cursor.

Slow sinusoidal ribbons of light that undulate across the field like an aurora and bend toward the pointer — a calm, luminous canvas backdrop.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/aurora-ribbon`

Props:
- `bands`: number (default 3) — Number of ribbons, clamped to 1–6.
- `height`: number (default 320) — Stage height in px when used standalone.
- `children`: ReactNode — Overlay content rendered above the field.

Notes:
- Ribbons bloom where they overlap (additive compositing); the loop pauses offscreen and when the tab is hidden.
- Under reduced motion it renders one static frame — no undulation, no pointer response.

### Point Globe (KQ-050) — `point-globe`
A dotted planet you spin.

A globe of points on a Fibonacci sphere that drifts on its own and spins under your drag, carrying angular momentum — near points brighten, the far side dims.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/point-globe`

Props:
- `points`: number (default 520) — Dots on the sphere, clamped to 120–700.
- `autoRotate`: boolean (default true) — Idle drift when not being dragged.
- `height`: number (default 340) — Stage height in px.

Notes:
- Drag to spin; release carries momentum that decays back to the idle drift. The loop pauses offscreen and when hidden.
- Under reduced motion it renders a static globe — no drift, no momentum.

### Voronoi Shatter (KQ-051) — `voronoi-shatter`
Tap and the tessellation shatters, then reseals.

A Voronoi tessellation that fractures outward from your tap — shards fly, rotate, and fade, then ring back into place and reseal on the recoil spring.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/voronoi-shatter`

Props:
- `cells`: number (default 28) — Voronoi seed sites, clamped to 8–60.
- `height`: number (default 280) — Stage height in px.
- `aria-label`: string — Names the surface.

Notes:
- Cells are a real Voronoi built from half-plane clipping, computed once; the shard transforms repaint imperatively from one progress spring.
- Under reduced motion a tap leaves the tessellation whole — no shatter.

### Comet Cursor (KQ-052) — `comet-cursor`
A comet that chases the pointer.

A contained field where the pointer drags a luminous comet — a bright head with a tapering tail that thickens with speed and collapses when you rest.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/comet-cursor`

Props:
- `trail`: number (default 28) — Trail sample count, clamped to 8–64.
- `height`: number (default 320) — Stage height in px.
- `children`: ReactNode — Overlay content rendered above the field.

Notes:
- The tail widens with pointer speed and collapses into the head at rest; the loop pauses offscreen and when hidden.
- Under reduced motion it renders one static frame — no trail, no pointer response.

### Ripple Surface (KQ-053) — `ripple-surface`
Tap and wavefronts interfere.

Tap the surface and a wavefront propagates out; tap again and the rings overlap into visible interference — a calm canvas pond that answers every touch.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/ripple-surface`

Props:
- `height`: number (default 320) — Stage height in px.
- `children`: ReactNode — Overlay content rendered above the field.

Notes:
- Concurrent ripples are pooled and capped; crests reinforce where wavefronts cross. The loop pauses offscreen and when hidden.
- Under reduced motion it renders one calm static frame — a tap does not propagate.

### Flow Diagram (KQ-054) — `flow-diagram`
Quantity, flowing between nodes.

A Sankey flow — nodes in columns, links sized to their value with a marching-dash current, and a hover that traces a node's whole upstream and downstream path.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/flow-diagram`

Props:
- `nodes`: { id: string; label: string; column: number }[] — Graph nodes with their column placement.
- `links`: { source: string; target: string; value: number }[] — Flows; value drives link thickness.
- `height`: number (default 300) — Stage height in px.

Notes:
- Hover or focus a node to trace its connected path; an sr-only summary lists every flow.
- Under reduced motion the dashes stop marching — the highlight is color only.

### Timeline Spine (KQ-055) — `timeline-spine`
A playhead that rides your scroll.

A scroll-linked timeline — a playhead rides the spine as you scroll, the spine fills behind it, and each event pops in as the head reaches it.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/timeline-spine`

Props:
- `events`: { title: string; detail?: ReactNode; marker?: ReactNode }[] — Ordered events rendered along the spine.
- `aria-label`: string — Names the timeline region.

Notes:
- Events are a semantic ordered list, readable regardless of motion; the spine and playhead are decorative.
- Under reduced motion everything renders in its final state — no scroll-driven animation.

### Pull to Refresh (KQ-056) — `pull-to-refresh`
Pull past the detent; rows cascade in.

Over-pull the list past a calibrated detent and release — it holds to refresh, then settles back as the fresh rows cascade in.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/pull-to-refresh`

Props:
- `onRefresh`: () => Promise<void> | void — Called when a past-detent pull is released.
- `children`: ReactNode — The scrollable content.
- `threshold`: number (default 72) — Pixels past which release triggers a refresh.

Notes:
- Only engages when the list is scrolled to the top; a focusable Refresh button keeps it operable without a pointer.
- Under reduced motion the pull eases home without overshoot and the new rows fade in once — no cascade.

### Orbit Menu (KQ-057) — `orbit-menu`
A dial of options that snaps to a detent.

Items ring a hub and rotate on a weighted dial — flick the ring and it flings, then snaps the nearest item into the active detent at the top.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/orbit-menu`

Props:
- `items`: { id: string; label: string; icon?: ReactNode }[] — Options placed evenly around the ring.
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: string / string / (id) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled active item.
- `size`: number (default 260) — Ring diameter in px.

Notes:
- Drag to spin, click an item, or arrow-key to rotate; a live region announces the active item.
- Under reduced motion selection snaps to the detent instantly — no fling momentum.

### Spark Burst (KQ-058) — `spark-burst`
Restraint as celebration.

A calibrated celebration, not confetti — call fire() and a tight radial burst of hairline rays and a fast ring shoots out and fades, monochrome with a signal glint.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/spark-burst`

Props:
- `ref.fire(opts?)`: (opts?: { rays?: number }) => void — Imperative handle — call it to emit one burst.
- `rays / spread`: number / number — Ray count (default 12) and travel distance in px (default 40).
- `color`: string (default "var(--signal)") — Ray color.

Notes:
- Get a ref of type SparkBurstHandle and call fire(); rapid fires stack and clean themselves up.
- Under reduced motion fire() gives a single centered pulse — no radiating rays.

### Heart Tap (KQ-059) — `heart-tap`
Pop, sparks, and a rolling count.

A like that celebrates — the heart squashes then pops, a tight ring of sparks bursts once, and the count rolls up; un-liking deflates and rolls back down.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/heart-tap`

Props:
- `liked / defaultLiked / onChange`: boolean / boolean / (state) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled liked state; onChange gets { liked, count }.
- `count / defaultCount`: number / number — Controlled or uncontrolled count, clamped at zero.
- `size`: number (default 28) — Heart glyph size in px.

Notes:
- A real toggle button — Space/Enter like, aria-pressed and the count are announced.
- Under reduced motion the fill switches and the count swaps instantly — no pop, sparks, or roll.

### Reaction Fly (KQ-060) — `reaction-fly`
Reactions that bloom, then fly.

Press to bloom a reaction picker; choosing one sends a copy flying up — rising, drifting, and fading on its own jittered path — while its tally ticks up.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/reaction-fly`

Props:
- `reactions`: { id: string; label: string; node?: ReactNode }[] — The pickable reactions; defaults to a curated set.
- `onReact`: (id: string) => void — Fires with the reaction id on each selection.
- `aria-label`: string — Names the trigger.

Notes:
- Full menu semantics — the trigger has aria-haspopup, items are menuitems, Escape closes, and each reaction is announced.
- Under reduced motion the picker opens without stagger and selecting bumps the tally with no fly-up.

### Facet Cube (KQ-071) — `facet-cube`
Six faces, one cube, snap detents.

A six-face content cube with real CSS geometry — drag to rotate with per-pixel resistance, release and it snaps both axes to the nearest 90° detent, or walk faces with the arrow keys; the facing face is announced on settle.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/facet-cube`

Props:
- `faces`: ReactNode[] — Up to six face plates; missing faces render empty.
- `labels`: string[] — Per-face names for the screen-reader announcement.
- `size`: number (default 220) — Cube edge in px; the wrapper pads for mid-turn corners.
- `defaultFace / onFaceChange`: number / (index) => void — Starting face and the settle callback.

Notes:
- A focusable group with aria-roledescription — arrows rotate one detent, Home returns to face one, and the facing face is announced politely.
- Under reduced motion the cube renders flat: one face at a time, instant swaps, same keys and announcements.

### Prism Flip (KQ-072) — `prism-flip`
Three panels on one rolling prism.

A triangular prism that rolls toward the viewer between three panels — tap to advance, drag vertically against the detents, or step with the arrow keys; true equilateral geometry with edge shading.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/prism-flip`

Props:
- `panels`: ReactNode[] — The three faces; extra entries are ignored.
- `index / defaultIndex / onIndexChange`: number / number / (index) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled facing panel.
- `labels`: string[] — Per-panel names for the announcement.
- `height`: number (default 128) — Panel height in px; clearance is derived from it.

Notes:
- A focusable rotator — arrows step panels, Home returns to the first, and the facing panel is announced politely.
- Under reduced motion the prism renders one flat panel with instant crossfade swaps.

### Coin Toggle (KQ-073) — `coin-toggle`
Heads on, tails off.

A switch struck as a coin — it flips 180° in true perspective on every toggle, always forward, and lands with an edge-wobble on the recoil spring; faces are yours to mint.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/coin-toggle`

Props:
- `checked / defaultChecked / onCheckedChange`: boolean / boolean / (checked) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled switch state.
- `size`: number (default 56) — Coin diameter in px.
- `faces`: { on: ReactNode; off: ReactNode } — Custom face artwork; defaults to minted glyphs.
- `aria-label`: string — Required accessible name for the bare switch.

Notes:
- A real role=switch button — Space and Enter flip it, aria-checked tracks state, and focus-visible rings are never removed.
- Under reduced motion the faces crossfade with no flip or wobble — the accent change carries the state.

### Dice Roll (KQ-074) — `dice-roll`
A die that always answers.

Hold to charge — the die swells and shivers while an arc fills — then release to tumble two full turns and land on a face you chose: outcomes come from a prop or a fixed sequence, never chance.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/dice-roll`

Props:
- `value / onRoll`: number / (value) => void — Controlled outcome for the next roll, and the landing callback.
- `sequence`: number[] — Uncontrolled outcomes, cycled in order — deterministic by design.
- `size`: number (default 88) — Die edge in px.

Notes:
- A real button — hold Space or Enter to charge, release to roll; Escape aborts a charge; the result is announced politely.
- Under reduced motion there is no shiver or tumble — release swaps straight to the outcome face and announces it.

### Gimbal Dial (KQ-075) — `gimbal-dial`
Two rings, two channels, one hub.

A two-axis picker built as nested rings in perspective — spin the outer band for one value and the inner for the other; flicks carry momentum, releases settle on detents, and a hub reads both channels live.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/gimbal-dial`

Props:
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: { yaw, pitch } / same / (value) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled 0–100 per axis; fires once per settled detent.
- `step`: number (default 5) — Detent size in value units.
- `size`: number (default 200) — Outer ring diameter in px.
- `axisLabels`: { yaw: string; pitch: string } — Accessible names for the two slider bands.

Notes:
- Each ring is an independent role=slider — arrows step it, Home and End jump, and aria-valuenow tracks the detents.
- Under reduced motion the rings render flat and drags update values directly with no momentum.

### Rolodex List (KQ-076) — `rolodex-list`
A wheel of records with a crest.

Records on a vertical wheel — drag, scroll a notch at a time, or arrow through; cards flip over the crest, the front record carries the accent, and Enter pulls it. A selection control, not a display drum.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/rolodex-list`

Props:
- `items`: { id, content, label? }[] — The records on the wheel.
- `index / defaultIndex / onIndexChange`: number / number / (index) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled front record; change fires on settle.
- `onSelect`: (id, index) => void — Explicit activation — Enter, Space, or a tap on the front card.
- `height / loop`: number / boolean (default 260 / true) — Viewport height in px, and whether the wheel wraps.

Notes:
- A real listbox — aria-activedescendant tracks the front record, arrows rotate one step, and the wheel only captures scroll while the pointer is over it.
- Under reduced motion it renders a flat three-row list with instant swaps and the same semantics.

### Wheel Picker (KQ-077) — `wheel-picker`
Options on a drum, settled by detent.

A value picker built as a recessed drum — options curve beneath a fixed selection window, drags carry momentum into detents, every landing ticks the window caps, and letter keys typeahead straight to an option.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/wheel-picker`

Props:
- `options`: { value: string; label: string }[] — The drum's rows.
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: string / string / (value) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled selection; fires once per settle.
- `visibleRows / height`: 3 | 5 | 7 / number (default 5 / 200) — Window size and control height.
- `aria-label / aria-labelledby`: string — One is required — it is a bare form control.

Notes:
- A real listbox — arrows and Page keys step rows, Home and End jump, letters typeahead, and the wheel only captures scroll over the control.
- Under reduced motion the drum renders as a flat column with instant swaps and the same window styling.

### Flip Mosaic (KQ-078) — `flip-mosaic`
One grid, two boards, a traveling flip.

Two boards on one grid — every tile carries an A face and a B face, and toggling sweeps a cascade of Y-flips across the mosaic from your chosen origin, always inside the choreography budget.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/flip-mosaic`

Props:
- `tiles`: { a: ReactNode; b: ReactNode }[] — Each tile's two faces.
- `side / defaultSide / onSideChange`: "a" | "b" / same / (side) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled facing board.
- `origin`: "start" | "end" | "center" (default "start") — Where the cascade wave begins.
- `columns / tileAspect / gap`: number / number / number (default 4 / 1 / 8) — Grid shape.

Notes:
- Interactive mode is a single role=switch button — Space and Enter flip the board and the settled side is announced.
- Under reduced motion there is no cascade or 3D — the whole board crossfades at once.

### Orrery (KQ-079) — `orrery`
An orbital system with a focus slot.

Bodies orbit a hub on inclined elliptical paths — inner rings run faster, the whole system pauses when unwatched, and clicking a body flies it forward into a focus card until Escape sends it home.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/orrery`

Props:
- `items`: { id, label, node? }[] — Up to eight orbiting bodies; default faces are minted discs.
- `hub / detail`: ReactNode / (item) => ReactNode — The center piece and the focus-card renderer.
- `radius / inclineDeg / period`: number / number / number (default 120 / 24 / 36) — Orbit geometry and the outer revolution time in seconds.
- `onFocusChange`: (id | null) => void — Fires when a body is captured or released.

Notes:
- Every body is a real button — Enter captures it, Escape releases, and captures are announced politely; the orbit pauses under keyboard focus.
- The loop pauses offscreen and when the tab hides, with the clock rebased on resume; under reduced motion the system rests at its phase positions and captures swap instantly.

### Balance Mobile (KQ-080) — `balance-mobile`
Torque you can tug.

A hanging mobile with honest torque — pods hang from beams whose tilt is computed from the loads beneath them; pull a pod and every beam above re-tilts live, then the whole rig swings back through equilibrium on release.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/balance-mobile`

Props:
- `items`: { id, label, weight? }[] — Up to four pods; weight defaults to one.
- `height`: number (default 260) — Rig height in px.

Notes:
- Every pod is a real button — Space or Enter gives it a tug, announced politely; drags add weight proportional to the pull.
- Under reduced motion the rig rests at equilibrium and tugs read as a small instant tilt with no swing.

### Ring Dial (KQ-111) — `ring-dial`
Stops that pull like magnets.

A lens ring in perspective — a knurled band whose detents pull the value in like magnets as you cross them; the fixed needle blinks on every land and the hub pulses the committed stop.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/ring-dial`

Props:
- `min / max / detents / step`: number / number / number[] / number (default 0 / 100 / — / 10) — Range and stops; explicit detents win over the step grid.
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange / onInput`: number / number / (value) => void / (value) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled value; change fires per land, input streams live.
- `format`: (value) => string — Formats the hub readout, band numerals, and aria-valuetext.
- `size`: number (default 220) — Dial diameter in px.

Notes:
- A real slider — arrows step between detents, Home and End reach the first and last, and aria-valuetext speaks the formatted stop.
- Under reduced motion the ring renders flat and tracks the pointer one-to-one with no magnetic pull; the needle blink remains as the land cue.

### Z Accordion (KQ-112) — `z-accordion`
Sections that step back, not down.

An accordion that spends depth instead of height — the open sheet glides to the front while the rest step back into the rack, scaled, dimmed, and stacked so every header stays in reach.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/z-accordion`

Props:
- `items`: { id, title, content }[] — The sheets; three to five read best.
- `open / defaultOpen / onOpenChange`: string / string / (id) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled open sheet — exactly one at a time.

Notes:
- True accordion semantics — header buttons carry aria-expanded, panels are labelled regions, and arrows, Home, and End move between headers.
- Under reduced motion it renders as a flat accordion in normal flow with instant expand and no recession.

### Depth Menu (KQ-113) — `depth-menu`
Branches recede; the trail hangs behind.

An inline menu that spends depth on hierarchy — entering a branch pushes the current level back into a hanging trail, a breadcrumb rail floats above for multi-level jumps, and focus always returns to the row you came from.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/depth-menu`

Props:
- `items`: { id, label, hint?, children? }[] — The tree; branch nodes carry children.
- `onSelect`: (id, path) => void — Fires on a leaf with its ancestor path.
- `depthGap`: number (default 80) — Perspective-honest recession per level.

Notes:
- WAI-ARIA menu keyboard — arrows move, Right or Enter descends, Left or Escape pops with focus returned, and level changes are announced.
- Under reduced motion levels swap instantly and the breadcrumb alone carries the context.

### Stage Tabs (KQ-114) — `stage-tabs`
Panels on a revolve.

Tabs staged like a theater revolve — the outgoing panel sweeps to one wing as the incoming one revolves in from the other, direction following tab order, over a floor shadow that squashes with the turn.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/stage-tabs`

Props:
- `tabs`: { id, label, content }[] — The tab set.
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: string / string / (id) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled active tab.
- `minHeight`: number (default 180) — Stage floor height in px.

Notes:
- Real tabs — roving tabindex, arrows auto-activate with wrap, Home and End jump, and the panel itself is focusable.
- Under reduced motion panels crossfade instantly and the underline jumps without gliding.

### Card Fan (KQ-115) — `card-fan`
Fan, sweep, play.

A hand of cards that fans open around a shared pivot — sweep to raise one, siblings part around it, and picking flies the card to a played slot where it lands on the recoil spring.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/card-fan`

Props:
- `cards`: { id, label, content }[] — Up to seven cards in the hand.
- `value / defaultValue / onSelect`: string | null / same / (id) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled played card.
- `open / defaultOpen / onOpenChange`: boolean / boolean / (open) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled fan state.
- `cardWidth`: number (default 120) — Card width in px; height is 1.45×.

Notes:
- The open hand is a radiogroup — arrows move the raised card, Enter or Space plays it, Escape folds the hand and returns focus to the stack.
- Under reduced motion the fan opens flat and instantly, raising is a highlight, and picks move without flight or bump.

### Hallway Menu (KQ-116) — `hallway-menu`
Every stop looks down its own hallway.

A mega-menu of parallel hallways — sweeping the rail looks down each corridor in one-point perspective, doors hinge off the walls toward a far-wall plate, and the whole view swings sideways between stops.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/hallway-menu`

Props:
- `hallways`: { id, label, items: { id, label, hint? }[] }[] — Two to five hallways on the rail.
- `onSelect`: (hallwayId, itemId) => void — Fires when a door is chosen.
- `corridorHeight`: number (default 220) — Corridor stage height in px.

Notes:
- Rail buttons carry aria-expanded; ArrowDown enters the corridor, arrows walk the doors in documented wall order, and Escape returns to the rail.
- Under reduced motion each corridor renders as a flat two-column panel with instant open and the same keyboard.

### Deck Switcher (KQ-117) — `deck-switcher`
Views raked back, one zooms forward.

An app-switcher for views — open the deck and every view rakes back into a tilted stack; slide the rake by drag, wheel, or arrows, then commit and the chosen view zooms forward while the rest fold away.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/deck-switcher`

Props:
- `views`: { id, title, content }[] — Two to six views; all stay mounted so their state survives switches.
- `activeId / defaultActiveId / onActiveChange`: string / string / (id) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled active view; fires only when the commit changes it.
- `height`: number (default 300) — Stage height in px.

Notes:
- The open deck is a listbox — arrows slide the rake, Home and End jump, Enter commits, and Escape cancels back to the previous view with focus returned.
- Under reduced motion the deck is a flat list of title cards and commits swap instantly.

### Flyover Map (KQ-118) — `flyover-map`
The page, miniature and tilted.

A scroll region with its own tilted minimap — districts sized to their sections, a live viewport indicator riding the scroll, and a click that flies the view to any district on the glide spring.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/flyover-map`

Props:
- `sections`: { id, label, content }[] — Three to six sections stacked in the scroll region.
- `onArrive`: (id) => void — Fires when a flight lands or scrolling settles in a new section.
- `height`: number (default 320) — Viewport height in px.

Notes:
- The minimap is a navigation landmark of real buttons — aria-current tracks the active district and arrivals are announced politely.
- Under reduced motion the map renders flat and district clicks jump instantly with no flight.

### Command Deck (KB-101) — `command-deck`
A palette that arms before it fires.

A command palette that arms before it fires — a deliberate fill sweeps the selected row before execution, so destructive commands never feel accidental.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/command-deck`

Props:
- `commands`: DeckCommand[] — { id, label, hint?, icon?, section?, keywords?, destructive?, run } per command.
- `open / defaultOpen / onOpenChange`: boolean / boolean / (open) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled visibility.
- `hotkey`: boolean (default true) — Global ⌘K / Ctrl+K toggle listener.
- `inline / portal`: boolean (default false / true) — Inline renders just the panel (docs/demos); portal overlays into body with focus trap and scroll lock.
- `recentKey`: string (default "kinetiq-deck-recents") — localStorage key for the five most recent command ids.

Notes:
- Full combobox semantics with focus trap and restore; Escape mid-arm cancels the sweep.
- Under reduced motion the arming sweep becomes an instant fill with a deliberate 120ms pause.

### Access Panel (KB-102) — `access-panel`
Sign-in that unlocks like a vault.

Sign-in that unlocks like a vault — steps ride a gantry rail, OTP digits drop into their cells, and the right code throws a breaker bolt open.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/access-panel`

Props:
- `expectedCode`: string (default "246810") — The six-digit code that throws the bolt.
- `onVerify`: (code: string) => void — Fires with the entered code on every attempt.
- `onComplete`: (email: string) => void — Fires once when the correct code lands.
- `email`: string — Prefills the email field.

Notes:
- One hidden input with autocomplete="one-time-code" drives the six visual cells — the accessibility-correct OTP pattern.
- Under reduced motion the rail slides become fades and the rejection nudge is skipped; the alert still announces.

### Signal Center (KB-103) — `signal-center`
An inbox that files itself.

An inbox that files itself — signals arrive on the conveyor, swipe away with real velocity, and clear-all sweeps the floor in a cascade.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/signal-center`

Props:
- `signals / defaultSignals / onSignalsChange`: Signal[] / Signal[] / (signals) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled feed; Signal = { id, source, title, detail?, time, read? }.
- `onArchive`: (signal: Signal) => void — Fires per archived signal, including clear-all.
- `title`: string (default "Signals") — Header title next to the live unread readout.

Notes:
- Archiving is keyboard-reachable via a per-row button; swiping is the fast path, not the only path.
- Under reduced motion arrivals and exits become fades and clear-all loses its stagger.

### Media Console (KB-104) — `media-console`
A media island that unfolds into a console.

A media island that unfolds into a console — the pill morphs open into transport, a caliper timeline, and a scope-scrubbed volume.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/media-console`

Props:
- `tracks`: Track[] — { id, title, artist, duration } — playback is simulated; the JSDoc shows wiring a real audio element.
- `defaultExpanded`: boolean (default false) — Start as the pill or the full console.

Notes:
- Complete keyboard transport; Escape collapses the console back to the pill.
- Under reduced motion the morph becomes a crossfade and the waveform holds still.

### Checkout Receipt (KB-105) — `checkout-receipt`
Payment that prints its proof.

Payment that prints its proof — hold to pay, then a receipt feeds out of the slot line by line, tears off, and takes a PAID stamp.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/checkout-receipt`

Props:
- `items`: ReceiptItem[] — Line items ({ name, price }) summed into the total.
- `currency`: string (default "$") — Price prefix.
- `onPay`: () => void — Fires when the hold completes.

Notes:
- The receipt is real text in a definition list; only the barcode is decorative.
- Under reduced motion the receipt appears whole and the stamp fades in.

### Intake Tray (KB-106) — `intake-tray`
Files land, physically.

Files land, physically — dropped files fall into the tray with a squash, shingle into a stack, fill their gauge rings, and slide to the processed rail.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/intake-tray`

Props:
- `onFiles`: (files: File[]) => void — Receives each accepted batch.
- `accept / maxFiles`: string / number (default — / 6) — Accepted types hint and tray capacity.
- `simulate`: boolean (default true) — Hash-driven demo progress; the JSDoc documents wiring real uploads.
- `ref`: Ref<IntakeTrayHandle> — Imperative addFiles() for programmatic intake.

Notes:
- The drop zone is a real button with a labeled native file input — fully keyboard operable.
- Under reduced motion chips stack neatly with fades; the gauge rings still fill.

### Readout Grid (KB-107) — `readout-grid`
Dashboard stats as instrument cards.

Dashboard stats as instrument cards — a carry-rolling counter, a self-drawing sparkline, a split-flap delta, and a needle gauge, mounted with one cascade.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/readout-grid`

Props:
- `metrics`: ReadoutGridMetrics — Optional full override of the four cards: counter, sparkline, flap, and gauge shapes.

Notes:
- Each card is an article whose label reads naturally to screen readers; chart visuals carry sr-only values.
- Under reduced motion cards render in place, the sparkline arrives fully drawn, and the needle jumps.

### Launch Checklist (KB-108) — `launch-checklist`
Setup steps that stamp themselves done.

Onboarding checklist where ticks draw themselves, finished steps strike through and settle to the bottom, and completing the set lands a CALIBRATED stamp.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/launch-checklist`

Props:
- `steps`: ChecklistStep[] — Step definitions: id, title, optional description.
- `completed / defaultCompleted`: string[] — Controlled or uncontrolled set of completed step ids.
- `onCompletedChange`: (completed: string[]) => void — Fires on every toggle with the new completed set.
- `onComplete`: () => void — Fires once when the final step completes.

Notes:
- Rows are native checkboxes under the hood — the whole block is keyboard and screen-reader operable.
- Under reduced motion rows reorder instantly and the stamp fades in; the progress track still reports value via ARIA.

### Iris Menu (KB-109) — `iris-menu`
Actions bloom from where you pressed.

Actions bloom from where you pressed — items launch from the trigger's center to seats along the freest quadrant's arc, and fold back in on close.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/iris-menu`

Props:
- `items`: IrisMenuItem[] — Up to six: { id, icon, label, onSelect, disabled?, destructive? }.
- `placement`: "auto" | quadrant (default "auto") — Auto measures the viewport and blooms into the roomiest quadrant.
- `radius`: number (default 84) — Distance from trigger center to each seat.

Notes:
- Arrow keys rotate focus around the ring; Escape closes and restores the trigger.
- Under reduced motion items fade in already seated — no travel.

### Overflow Rail (KB-110) — `overflow-rail`
More actions, sprung from the dots.

More actions, sprung from the dots — the pill rail morphs open inline while primaries yield with a recoil part and secondaries cascade in.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/overflow-rail`

Props:
- `primary / secondary`: RailAction[] — Always-visible actions and the set revealed by the ⋯ trigger.
- `open / defaultOpen / onOpenChange`: boolean / boolean / (open) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled expansion.
- `label`: string (default "Actions") — Accessible toolbar name.

Notes:
